UF-111-1.title: QA upgraded from UNDECIDABLE to NO_GO (sharper obstruction visible from KNY paste)
UF-111-1.description: Round-1 QA was UNDECIDABLE_ROUTE_A pending KNY substrate. Round-2 paste of KNY §8.5.17 + Okamoto §1 surfaced the structural obstruction: KNY (8.237) is a normalized representative of H_D6^(1), and coefficient-matching against the Okamoto WLOG slice eta_0=eta_inf=1 produces only the (theta_0, theta_inf) ↔ (a_1, a_2) sector. The unnormalized (eta_inf, eta_0) directions — which the project's V_quad image (1/6, 0, 0, -1/2) lives in — cannot be recovered from KNY (8.237) alone.
UF-111-1.agent_action: PERFORM ~30-min KNY §8.5 surrounding-sections scan (sections beyond §8.5.17) before treating NO_GO_ROUTE_A as final. If un-normalization map for H_D6^(1) found in surrounding KNY §8 prose, QA could revive to UNDECIDABLE and require re-fire.
UF-111-1.next_step: Optional pre-069r3-B sanity scan; not blocking 069r3-B dispatch since Route B does not depend on Route A status.

UF-111-2.title: Route D value proposition strengthened by round-2 outcome
UF-111-2.description: Round-1 framed Route D as 'fragile solo path' (numerical fitting alone, no analytic backing). Round-2 outcome — Route B = GO_CONDITIONAL gated on FW Prop 4.1 mechanism (a) pull-back — turns Route D into 'high-value parallel verification of mechanism (a)'. A numerical fit of the chart-map at V_quad's image should reproduce the -1/3 offset if mechanism (a) is correct.
UF-111-2.agent_action: Schedule QD round-2 substrate-paste BEFORE 069r3-B fires (synth Caveat 3). If QD lands GO_ROUTE_D, dispatch 069r3-B (analytic) and 069r3-D (numerical) in parallel; cross-validation lowers cascade risk to Route C / Route F.
UF-111-2.next_step: Draft 069r2-QD-round2-substrate-paste-request.md (mirror of 108 deposit's operator_substrate_paste_request.md, scoped to V_quad numerical-solution structure + (α, β) extraction operation spec).

UF-111-3.title: Mechanism (a) Hamiltonian-shift hypothesis structurally unique among the three -1/3 candidates
UF-111-3.description: Synth analysis: mechanism (a) (FW Prop 4.1 auxiliary Hamiltonian h = tH + (1/4)v_1^2 - (1/2)t) is uniquely positioned among the three -1/3 candidates because it (i) closes Route B without additional acquisition, (ii) has primary-literature anchor (FW eq. (4.3)), (iii) keeps the chart-map within σ-form / Hamiltonian P_III machinery (no HALT-S5 Route F surfacing). Mechanism (b) (-1/12 fall-back) is project-internal calibration only. Mechanism (c) (Sakai D_6^(1)) is a Route F surfacing.
UF-111-3.agent_action: Draft 069r3_route_b_executor_envelope.txt with mechanism (a) pull-back as primary executor task and -1/3 offset trace as primary acceptance criterion. Encode mechanism (b) and (c) fallbacks as explicit branches in §5 verdict template.
UF-111-3.next_step: 069r3-B executor envelope drafting (synth recommendation: 5-10 hr executor effort).

UF-111-4.title: Search's-Fatigue immunity: QA NO_GO + QB.4 GO_CONDITIONAL + QE TRIVIAL is a structurally honest split, not a closure-by-default
UF-111-4.description: The three round-2 verdicts — QA=NO_GO, QB.4=GO_CONDITIONAL, QE=TRIVIAL with mechanism deferral — could superficially read as 'closure achieved' (Route B unblocked, governance discharged). The actual epistemic state is sharper: Route B is conditional on a non-trivial executor computation (5-10 hr FW Prop 4.1 pull-back) that has not been performed, and the -1/3 offset mechanism is open across three candidates with mechanism (a) only structurally favored, not verified. The synth's caveats explicitly mark this asymmetry: §3.5.1 records WHAT is open; 069r3-B closes it.
UF-111-4.agent_action: When drafting 069r3-B executor envelope, ensure spec language preserves the asymmetry: mechanism (a) is a hypothesis, not a foregone conclusion; pull-back computation could fail or route through (c).
UF-111-4.next_step: Reflected in 069r3-B envelope drafting language; no separate action.
